summary: Generate mode testing complete for file-based product cache system. All backend tests pass (47 tests), all frontend tests pass (33 tests). File-based cache is working correctly - products persist even after process restarts.

cache_status_endpoint.notes: GET /api/wc/cache/status returns file-based cache info with 9 cache files for en/el/it languages and all/ready-florarium/diy-kit product types

cache_invalidate_endpoint.notes: POST /api/wc/cache/invalidate refreshes cache from WooCommerce (may timeout on slow connections)

products_from_cache.notes: GET /api/wc/products returns products from file cache, not empty

english_products.notes: GET /api/wc/products?lang=en returns 6 products with all required fields

greek_products.notes: GET /api/wc/products?lang=el returns 6 products from file cache

product_required_fields.notes: All products have required fields: id, name, price, image, category

gallery_section.notes: Products display correctly in TerrariumGallery section with name, price (€), and add to cart

diy_kits_section.notes: DIY kits display correctly with proper pricing (€34.99 for starter kit)

seed_data_creation: Tests use unique UUIDs/timestamps for session IDs. All test data uses TEST_ prefix.
context_for_next_testing_agent: File-based cache system fully tested: 1) Cache files stored in /app/backend/product_cache/*.json with format {lang}_{product_type}.json. 2) GET /api/wc/cache/status returns all cache files with counts. 3) POST /api/wc/cache/invalidate refreshes from WooCommerce (slow operation, may timeout). 4) Products endpoint serves from file cache first, falls back to WooCommerce API on cache miss. 5) Checkout flow updated - now requires clicking 'Buy Without Registration' before accessing billing form. Test specs updated to handle this new flow.
